From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Church of England ecclesiastical province

| Province of York | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Church | [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England") |
| [Metropolitan bishop](/wiki/Metropolitan_bishop "Metropolitan bishop") | [Archbishop of York](/wiki/Archbishop_of_York "Archbishop of York") |
| [Cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral") | [York Minster](/wiki/York_Minster "York Minster") |
| [Dioceses](/wiki/Dioceses "Dioceses") | 12 |

The **Province of York**, or less formally the **Northern Province**, is one of two [ecclesiastical provinces](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_province "Ecclesiastical province") making up the [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England") and consists of 14 [dioceses](/wiki/Diocese "Diocese") which cover the northern third of [England](/wiki/England "England") and the [Isle of Man](/wiki/Isle_of_Man "Isle of Man").[[1]](#cite_note-1) York was elevated to an archbishopric in AD 735: [Ecgbert](/wiki/Ecgbert_of_York "Ecgbert of York") was the first [archbishop](/wiki/Archbishop_of_York "Archbishop of York"). At one time, the archbishops of York also claimed metropolitan authority over [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), but these claims were never realised and ceased when the [Archdiocese of St Andrews](/wiki/Archdiocese_of_St_Andrews "Archdiocese of St Andrews") was established.

The province's [metropolitan bishop](/wiki/Metropolitan_bishop "Metropolitan bishop") is the [archbishop of York](/wiki/Archbishop_of_York "Archbishop of York") (the junior of the Church of England's two archbishops). [York Minster](/wiki/York_Minster "York Minster") serves as the [mother church](/wiki/Mother_Church#Cathedral "Mother Church") of the Province of York.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Boundary changes since the mid-19th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Province_of_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Boundary changes since the mid-19th century")]

In 1836, the diocese of [Ripon](/wiki/Diocese_of_Ripon "Diocese of Ripon") was formed (Diocese of Ripon and Leeds from 1999 until 2014), followed by further foundations: [Manchester](/wiki/Anglican_Diocese_of_Manchester "Anglican Diocese of Manchester") in 1847, [Liverpool](/wiki/Anglican_Diocese_of_Liverpool "Anglican Diocese of Liverpool") in 1880, [Newcastle](/wiki/Diocese_of_Newcastle "Diocese of Newcastle") in 1882, [Wakefield](/wiki/Diocese_of_Wakefield "Diocese of Wakefield") in 1888, [Sheffield](/wiki/Diocese_of_Sheffield "Diocese of Sheffield") in 1914, [Bradford](/wiki/Diocese_of_Bradford "Diocese of Bradford") in 1919, [Blackburn](/wiki/Diocese_of_Blackburn "Diocese of Blackburn") in 1926, and [Leeds](/wiki/Anglican_Diocese_of_Leeds "Anglican Diocese of Leeds") in 2014.

The [Diocese of Southwell](/wiki/Diocese_of_Southwell "Diocese of Southwell") (Southwell and Nottingham since 2005) was a special case: in 1837, the [archdeaconry of Nottingham](/wiki/Archdeaconry_of_Nottingham "Archdeaconry of Nottingham"), which until then had formed part of the [Diocese of York](/wiki/Diocese_of_York "Diocese of York"), was transferred to the [Diocese of Lincoln](/wiki/Diocese_of_Lincoln "Diocese of Lincoln") and hence to the [Province of Canterbury](/wiki/Province_of_Canterbury "Province of Canterbury"). In 1884, the counties of [Nottinghamshire](/wiki/Nottinghamshire "Nottinghamshire") and [Derbyshire](/wiki/Derbyshire "Derbyshire") were merged to form the new Diocese of Southwell. The [Diocese of Derby](/wiki/Diocese_of_Derby "Diocese of Derby") was created in 1927, removing Derbyshire from Southwell's jurisdiction, and in 1935 the diocese of Southwell was transferred back to the province of York.
